prompt_text:
Analyze the following python source code for violations of the Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP).

Use a TWO-STAGE reasoning process:

## Stage 1 — List design smells (silent, for your own reasoning)
Identify relevant low-level design smells you observe in the code:
- God Class, Feature Envy, Shotgun Surgery, Divergent Change
- Long Method, Large Class, Long Parameter List
- Refused Bequest, Inappropriate Intimacy, Message Chains
- Switch Statements on type, Data Clumps
Think through these int...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "seaborn/matrix.py",
      "entity_name": "ClusterGrid",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 638,
      "line_end": 705,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Subclass fails to provide the 'axes' attribute required by base class methods",
      "reasoning": "Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because ClusterGrid inherits from Grid (axisgrid.py), but its __init__ method fails to define the 'axes' attribute. The base class method Grid.add_legend (axisgrid.py, line 150) explicitly relies on self.axes.flat[0]. Consequently, ClusterGrid is not substitutable for Grid, as calling add_legend on a ClusterGrid instance will result in an AttributeError."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "seaborn/categorical.py",
      "entity_name": "_CategoricalPlotter.plot_swarms",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 466,
      "line_end": 491,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Monkey-patching the 'draw' method of a base type instance alters its behavioral contract",
      "reasoning": "Inappropriate Intimacy smell -> LSP violation because this method replaces the 'draw' method of a matplotlib.collections.PathCollection instance with a custom local function. This violates the behavioral contract of the matplotlib Artist hierarchy; any consumer expecting a standard PathCollection will now trigger Seaborn-specific beeswarm logic during the rendering pass, which is an unexpected side effect for that type."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "seaborn/distributions.py",
      "entity_name": "_DistributionPlotter._add_legend",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 144,
      "line_end": 155,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Type-checking (isinstance) used to branch logic instead of polymorphic dispatch",
      "reasoning": "Switch Statements on type smell -> LSP violation because the method uses isinstance(ax_obj, mpl.axes.Axes) to determine how to add a legend. This breaks polymorphism; if a new subtype of a grid or axes is introduced that doesn't inherit from the specific classes checked, the logic fails or defaults to an incorrect assumption (the 'else' block assuming FacetGrid API)."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "seaborn/categorical.py",
      "entity_name": "_CategoricalAggPlotter",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 1032,
      "line_end": 1034,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Subclass overrides class attributes used for data mapping, changing base class contract",
      "reasoning": "Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because _CategoricalAggPlotter overrides the 'flat_structure' attribute defined in _CategoricalPlotter. This attribute is used by the base class VectorPlotter to define how data is assigned to variables. By changing this mapping from {'y': '@values'} to {'x': '@index', 'y': '@values'}, the subclass alters the fundamental data-handling contract of the base type."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "seaborn/categorical.py",
      "entity_name": "_CategoricalPlotter.__init__",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 73,
      "line_end": 110,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Subclass mutates base class state and structure in a way that breaks mapping invariants",
      "reasoning": "Inappropriate Intimacy smell -> LSP violation because the subclass constructor mutates the 'plot_data' and 'variables' state established by the base class VectorPlotter. Specifically, it renames columns (line 80) and injects dummy data (line 104) to force categorical rules. This breaks the post-conditions of the base class initialization, as the resulting object no longer adheres to the standard variable mapping contract expected by other VectorPlotter methods."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Subclass fails to provide the 'axes' attribute required by base class methods
findings.reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because ClusterGrid inherits from Grid (axisgrid.py), but its __init__ method fails to define the 'axes' attribute. The base class method Grid.add_legend (axisgrid.py, line 150) explicitly relies on self.axes.flat[0]. Consequently, ClusterGrid is not substitutable for Grid, as calling add_legend on a ClusterGrid instance will result in an AttributeError.

findings.description: Monkey-patching the 'draw' method of a base type instance alters its behavioral contract
findings.reasoning: Inappropriate Intimacy smell -> LSP violation because this method replaces the 'draw' method of a matplotlib.collections.PathCollection instance with a custom local function. This violates the behavioral contract of the matplotlib Artist hierarchy; any consumer expecting a standard PathCollection will now trigger Seaborn-specific beeswarm logic during the rendering pass, which is an unexpected side effect for that type.

findings.description: Type-checking (isinstance) used to branch logic instead of polymorphic dispatch
findings.reasoning: Switch Statements on type smell -> LSP violation because the method uses isinstance(ax_obj, mpl.axes.Axes) to determine how to add a legend. This breaks polymorphism; if a new subtype of a grid or axes is introduced that doesn't inherit from the specific classes checked, the logic fails or defaults to an incorrect assumption (the 'else' block assuming FacetGrid API).

findings.description: Subclass overrides class attributes used for data mapping, changing base class contract
findings.reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because _CategoricalAggPlotter overrides the 'flat_structure' attribute defined in _CategoricalPlotter. This attribute is used by the base class VectorPlotter to define how data is assigned to variables. By changing this mapping from {'y': '@values'} to {'x': '@index', 'y': '@values'}, the subclass alters the fundamental data-handling contract of the base type.

findings.description: Subclass mutates base class state and structure in a way that breaks mapping invariants
findings.reasoning: Inappropriate Intimacy smell -> LSP violation because the subclass constructor mutates the 'plot_data' and 'variables' state established by the base class VectorPlotter. Specifically, it renames columns (line 80) and injects dummy data (line 104) to force categorical rules. This breaks the post-conditions of the base class initialization, as the resulting object no longer adheres to the standard variable mapping contract expected by other VectorPlotter methods.
